articles.title: Rukmini Dropped Heatwave Mode On - Gulte

articles.full_text:
Article by Kumar
Published on: 5:23 am, 28 February 2026
What makes this outfit stand out is the way it blends elegance with power. The rich red shade adds intensity, while the flowy fabric creates movement with every step she takes. The fitted drape around the waist enhances her frame, and the layered folds give the gown a stylish structure. Her minimal accessories allow the dress to remain the main focus. From the pose to the styling, everything about this look reflects confidence and star presence.
On the work front, Rukmini has two big movies lined up Dragon and Toxic. As per reports, she is set to share the screen with Tamil hero Dhruv Vikram in a love story directed by a young Telugu filmmaker. However, nothing has been made official yet.

articles.title: Tourists find 'blessing' in Arizona's winter heat wave - FOX 10 Phoenix
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat") Assam
articles.full_text:
The Phoenix area is experiencing its first day of 90-degree temperatures of the year, a milestone that coincides with major tourism and spring training events. FOX 10's Nicole Krasean has the details.
TEMPE, Ariz. - As the first day of 90 degrees or hotter arrives in the Valley, opinions are split: Some say it is too soon for the heat, while others are ready to warm up.
Local perspective:
The spike in temperature coincides with a peak travel season as visitors flock to Arizona for spring break, spring training, and the Extra Innings Festival. For those escaping harsh winters in the Midwest, the forecast is a welcome change.
"I absolutely love the heat, so bring it on," said one visitor. Another traveler noted that after a "freezing" stretch two weeks ago, they would take this weather any day of the week.
That sentiment was shared by a group from Cleveland who made the Desert Botanical Garden their first stop after landing Friday morning. Other visitors from Illinois and Minnesota spent the day hiking Hole in the Rock, describing the Arizona sunshine as a "blessing in disguise."
"We’ve been outside the whole time we were here, whether it was at the pool or sitting around the firepit last night enjoying the warmth," one hiker said.
A Hot Lineup:
In Tempe, music fans gathered at Tempe Beach Park for the Extra Innings Festival. While the lineup features plenty of country music and cowboy hats, some attendees—even those from desert climates—admitted the heat caught them off guard.
"It’s hot. It’s not even this hot in Vegas, so we’re a little scared," one festival-goer said.
